title: Judge NEEDS_REVISION returns cell to open with cleared verify; dispatch identity fixes
action: skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/cells.mjs recordJudgeVerdict (amended by advisor consult): on NEEDS_REVISION over a capped cell, set status open (NOT claimed), call releaseTrace (clears verify_passed/verify_command/verify_output), AND clear/reconcile the claims-store claim best-effort (clearClaim/releaseClaimFileBestEffort — capCell already deleted it, but reconcile defensively so no orphaned cross-session lock survives; see the hazard note at cells.mjs:1911-1921) while KEEPING the judge ledger entry and reopened_for_rework logging/decision. Post-rework path is proven live: capCell gates on verify_passed (L1185) and latest-judge-verdict (L1191), recordJudgeVerdict is append-only, so fresh verify + new PASS verdict re-cap cleanly. Tests in templates/tests/test_misc.mjs: update the ~L2619 test that locked in capped->claimed — assert open + cleared verify + preserved judge ledger; ADD (i) after NEEDS_REVISION reopen, capCell refuses until a FRESH verify is recorded; (ii) a DIFFERENT session id can claim the reopened cell with no claims-store orphan. skills/bee-hive/templates/lib/dispatch-prepare.mjs: use the validated worker name as the reservation identity in the rendered prompt instead of prepare-cellid (L109); for --force-ownership, if claims.mjs exposes a simple transfer primitive, transfer the claim under lock with the audit entry; if not simple, make the output state explicitly that ownership was NOT transferred (advisory) and file friction via bee backlog add.

truths: NEEDS_REVISION on a capped cell yields status open with verify evidence cleared and judge ledger intact
Re-cap after rework requires a fresh verify (stale verify_passed cannot satisfy the gate)
prohibitions: Do not drop the judge verdict ledger on reopen

trace.outcome: Judge NEEDS_REVISION now reopens a capped cell to open (not claimed) with claim+verify evidence cleared via releaseTrace, preserving the judge ledger and reopened_for_rework; the claims-store is reconciled defensively (best-effort) on that same transition, closing the stale-evidence re-cap hole. dispatch-prepare's rendered prompt now carries the validated worker name as the reservation identity instead of the synthetic prepare-<id> nickname; --force-ownership stays an explicit advisory-only bypass (no correct transfer primitive exists on the trace.worker ownership axis) and now says so in the returned envelope.
trace.verify_output: Ran each suite (the chain short-circuits at the first nonzero exit, so run individually for full evidence): test_misc.mjs 94 passed/1 failed, test_cells.mjs 98 passed/0 failed, test_cli_cells.mjs 24 passed/0 failed. The 3 new/updated D7 tests in test_misc.mjs (reopen->open+cleared-evidence+preserved-ledger; fresh-verify-required re-cap gate; different-session claim with no claims-store orphan) all PASS. The single test_misc.mjs failure is the pre-existing 'vendored source: templates vs .bee/bin byte-identical' guard, red on lib/cells.mjs — this is EXPECTED per plan.md ('.bee/bin/ and rendered trees sync at close-out D9') and per this cell's own action text ('may be red mid-swarm — that is the close-out cell's job'). Confirmed by direct test: attempting to copy templates/lib/cells.mjs over .bee/bin/lib/cells.mjs broke the live CLI (SyntaxError: lock.mjs does not export acquireStoreLockOnceSync, a dependency from a sibling cell not yet mirrored) — reverted immediately via git checkout, .bee/bin left untouched, CLI confirmed working again. dispatch-prepare.mjs was NOT previously drifted (byte-identical blob hash at HEAD) but is intentionally left unsynced too, for the same D9 reason. Verify scope per this cell is the misc/cells/cli_cells functional rows, all green.

checks.evidence: NEEDS_REVISION over capped sets status open, stamps reopened_for_rework + appends semantic_judge BEFORE releaseTrace clears worker/claim_session/verify fields; releaseClaimFileBestEffort reconciles claims-store.
test row: capCell refuses re-cap until a FRESH verify is recorded; honest reclaim->reverify->re-PASS still caps.
test row: a DIFFERENT session claims the reopened cell with no orphaned claim file.
dispatch-prepare.mjs:120 renders the validated worker as reservation identity; no prepare-<id> remains.
envelope carries transferred:false + explicit advisory note; friction row filed in backlog.
94 passed, 1 failed — only the vendored byte-identity row owned by close-out 1710-9.
pre-fix script re-capped on stale verify evidence; post-fix fails at first assertion (status open, verify_passed null).
fc71869 touches only the 3 scoped sources + bookkeeping; .bee/bin sync deliberately deferred to D9.
